report_name: FBI Warns 'Silent Ransom Group' (Luna Moth) is Sending Operatives In-Person to Steal Data

threat_actors.name: Luna Moth
threat_actors.description: A data extortion group, also known as Silent Ransom Group, that blends digital and physical intrusion methods. The group uses social engineering via phone calls and phishing to gain remote access. If remote access fails, they dispatch an operative to the victim's office to physically access a computer and exfiltrate data using a USB drive for extortion purposes.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft for extortion without encryption.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Phishing emails are used as an initial vector to direct victims to malicious sites or remote access software.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Access Software
attack_patterns.description: The group's primary goal during the social engineering phase is to have the victim install legitimate remote access software, which the attackers then use for command and control.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Voice
attack_patterns.description: The use of phone calls (vishing) to impersonate IT support is a key part of the group's social engineering strategy to trick employees.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: By gaining access to an employee's workstation through social engineering or physical access, the attackers operate within the context of a valid domain account.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: The core of the attack involves the physical operative collecting sensitive data from the local workstation they have gained access to.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Physical Medium
attack_patterns.description: The group uses USB drives or external hard drives to physically steal data from a victim's workstation after an operative gains in-person access.

tools.name: Remote Access Software
tools.description: The threat actor tricks victims into installing legitimate remote access software to gain control of their computers.

tools.name: robocopy
tools.description: The threat actor may use built-in utilities like robocopy to perform large file copy operations from workstations to USB storage devices for data exfiltration.

tools.name: xcopy
tools.description: The threat actor may use built-in utilities like xcopy to perform large file copy operations from workstations to USB storage devices for data exfiltration.

identities.name: Federal Bureau of Investigation
identities.description: Issued a formal alert about the evolving tactics of the Silent Ransom Group (Luna Moth).

identities.name: Law Firms
identities.description: A primary target sector for the Luna Moth group since 2023.

identities.name: Healthcare Organizations
identities.description: A sector targeted by the Luna Moth data extortion group.

identities.name: Insurance Organizations
identities.description: A sector targeted by the Luna Moth data extortion group.

identities.name: Financial Services Organizations
identities.description: A sector targeted by the Luna Moth data extortion group.

indicators.name: Suspicious File Copy to Removable Media
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious File Copy to Removable Media
id: 2b9c1a8e-4f5d-4b6c-8d1e-9a0b3c2d1e8f
status: experimental
description: 'Detects large file copy operations using robocopy or xcopy, which could be indicative of data exfiltration to a USB device as seen in Luna Moth attacks.'
references:
  - https://www.securitymagazine.com/articles/100580-fbi-warning-it-personnel-impersonated-by-cybercriminals
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-30
tags:
  - attack.t1052.001
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\robocopy.exe'
      - '\xcopy.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Monitors for the use of command-line utilities like robocopy or xcopy, which can be used to exfiltrate large amounts of data to removable media.

indicators.name: Anomalous Remote Access Tool Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Remote Access Tool Execution
id: 8c1d2e3f-4a5b-4c6d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: 'Detects the execution of common remote access tools like TeamViewer or AnyDesk, which may be installed by employees tricked by Luna Moth social engineering tactics.'
references:
  - https://www.securitymagazine.com/articles/100580-fbi-warning-it-personnel-impersonated-by-cybercriminals
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-30
tags:
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\TeamViewer.exe'
      - '\AnyDesk.exe'
  filter_legit_path:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_path
indicators.description: Monitors for the execution of remote access tools like TeamViewer and AnyDesk, which are used by the threat actor after successful social engineering.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious File Copy to Removable Media
